breaking_news.title: Interlock Ransomware Exploits Cisco FMC Zero-Day for Root Access
breaking_news.summary:
Interlock ransomware operators are actively exploiting a critical zero-day vulnerability in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center software. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-20131, allows unauthenticated remote attackers to gain root access through insecure deserialization of Java byte streams.

This incident poses a severe risk to organizations relying on Cisco firewalls for network security, potentially leading to widespread ransomware infections. Security teams must immediately apply patches or implement compensating controls to prevent unauthorized access and data encryption.

breaking_news.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit Deployed by State-Sponsored Hackers
breaking_news.summary:
State-sponsored hackers and spyware vendors are deploying a new iOS exploit kit named DarkSword to compromise devices running versions 18.4 through 18.7. The attack chain leverages six distinct vulnerabilities to bypass security controls and install surveillance payloads like GHOSTBLADE.

This proliferation of a sophisticated zero-day chain across multiple threat actors indicates a significant escalation in mobile surveillance capabilities. Apple users in targeted regions such as Saudi Arabia and Ukraine face heightened risks of full device compromise and data theft.

breaking_news.title: GlassWorm Malware Compromises 400+ Repositories in Supply Chain Attack
breaking_news.summary:
The GlassWorm malware campaign has compromised over 400 code repositories across GitHub, npm, and VSCode extensions. Attackers are using stolen tokens to force-push malicious code into popular Python projects and developer tools, creating a widespread supply chain threat.

This attack vector targets software developers directly, potentially infecting downstream applications and enterprise environments that depend on these compromised packages. Organizations must audit their dependencies and monitor for unauthorized changes to critical development libraries.

breaking_news.title: Stryker Medtech Giant Wiped by Handala Hacktivist Group
breaking_news.summary:
Medical technology giant Stryker suffered a massive cyberattack attributed to the pro-Palestinian hacktivist group Handala, wiping tens of thousands of employee devices. The attackers targeted the company's Microsoft environment without using traditional malware, forcing offices across 79 countries to shut down.

While medical devices remain safe, the disruption to electronic ordering systems highlights the vulnerability of critical healthcare infrastructure to state-aligned cyber operations. This event underscores the growing threat of identity weaponization and remote wiping tactics in the healthcare sector.

breaking_news.title: Critical Unpatched Telnetd Flaw Enables Remote Root Access
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers have disclosed a critical unpatched vulnerability in GNU InetUtils telnetd that enables unauthenticated remote code execution with root privileges. Tracked as CVE-2026-32746, the flaw stems from an out-of-bounds write in the LINEMODE handler affecting all versions up to 2.7.

Given the CVSS score of 9.8 and the lack of an immediate patch, systems running this legacy service are at extreme risk of complete compromise. Administrators are urged to disable telnetd or isolate affected systems until the expected April 1, 2026 patch is available.

breaking_news.title: AWS Bedrock AI Sandbox Flaw Allows Data Exfiltration
breaking_news.summary:
Security researchers discovered a flaw in AWS Bedrock AgentCore Code Interpreter that allows data exfiltration and remote command execution via DNS queries. The sandbox mode, intended to isolate AI workflows, fails to block outbound DNS traffic, creating a covert communication channel for attackers.

This vulnerability challenges the security assumptions of agentic AI environments and could lead to significant data leaks from cloud-based AI applications. AWS has acknowledged the report, but users should review their sandbox configurations to mitigate potential data exposure risks.

breaking_news.title: UK Companies House Flaw Exposed Millions of Business Records
breaking_news.summary:
The UK government agency Companies House confirmed a security flaw exposed details of millions of firms and allowed users to alter company records. The vulnerability in the WebFiling service was active since October 2025 before the service was temporarily taken offline for remediation.

This breach compromises the integrity of the national corporate registry, potentially enabling identity fraud and financial manipulation against registered businesses. Companies are advised to monitor their records for unauthorized changes and strengthen their digital identity protections.

breaking_news.title: CrackArmor Vulnerabilities Threaten 12 Million Linux Systems
breaking_news.summary:
Qualys researchers identified nine critical vulnerabilities in the Linux AppArmor security module that allow unprivileged users to gain root access. Known as CrackArmor, these flaws affect over 12 million enterprise systems running Ubuntu, Debian, and SUSE distributions by default.

Exploitation of these bugs could break container isolation and crash systems, posing a severe risk to cloud and IoT environments relying on AppArmor for mandatory access control. System administrators must prioritize patching these kernel-level vulnerabilities to prevent privilege escalation attacks.
